betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Plutonium-240§f

§aCommon but disappointing plutonium isotope — avoid in weapons-grade material!§f

§bPlutonium-240§f is a common and somewhat disappointing isotope. It isn't very useful on its own and contaminates weapons-grade plutonium.

§6Properties§f:
§b• Radioactivity§f: §e7.5§f RAD/s
§b• Proportion§f: §e22.2%§f of raw plutonium
§b• Category§f: Fertile

§6Production§f:
§b• Bred from§f Pu-239 neutron capture
§b• Indirect byproduct§f of Pu-239 production

§6Uses§f:
§b• Fuel§f: moderate role in plutonium fuel as §bReactor Grade Plutonium§f
§b• Breeding§f: Pu-240 + neutron → Pu-241 (if no fission occurs)
§b• Custom Nuke§f: §e1.05×§f multiplier to salted stage

§6Breeding Chain§f:
§b• Pu-240 → Nuclear Waste§f

§6Trivia§f:
§b• High spontaneous fission rate§f — prevents chain reaction in weapons. Must be minimized in warheads!
§b• Lower half-life§f than U-238 despite lower spontaneous fission rate

§eTip§f: Pu-240 is unavoidable in reactor-bred plutonium. To get weapons-grade material, use the §bSILEX§f to separate Pu-239 from Pu-240. Pu-240 can still be used in Reactor Grade Plutonium for fuel!
